articles.title: REVENUE
articles.summary: 98% of software vendors sell online, with over half generating the majority of of their revenue from ecommerce channels. But new research from Cleverbridge [4] shows that SaaS companies are lagging at checkout and beyond - leaving billions on the table.

articles.title: OPENAI LAUNCHES AN AI-POWERED BROWSER
articles.summary: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Atlas, an AI-powered browser, starting on macOS, with other platforms soon. The browser features built-in chat capabilities and a sidecar feature for seamless user interaction. It offers an AI agent for task automation but is limited to certain subscription tiers at launch.

articles.title: SESAME, THE CONVERSATIONAL AI STARTUP FROM OCULUS FOUNDERS, RAISES $250M AND LAUNCHES BETA
articles.summary: Sesame, founded by Oculus alumni, raised $250 million in Series B funding and launched a beta for its conversational AI app. The startup aims to embed AI agents into smart glasses with a natural voice interface. Sesame's tech is praised for its realistic dialogue, with its beta iOS app offering features like search and text interaction.

articles.title: LEAN STARTUP REBOOTED
articles.summary: For years, startups followed two playbooks: Lean Startup and Blitzscaling. Blitzscaling won once AI arrived and VCs flooded the space with capital. Now, as the AI bubble debate grows, the original lean approach is making a comeback.

articles.title: ONLY 100 METRICS MATTER
articles.summary: Companies track thousands of numbers, but almost none of them change decisions. In reality, about 100 metrics explain 90% of what's happening. Once you break the business into equations - growth, engagement, revenue - you see which levers actually matter. Everything else lives in the long tail: useful for edge cases, not for running the company.

articles.title: WHY WE BUY: THE OMISSION BIAS
articles.summary: People judge harmful actions more harshly than harmful inactions - this is known as the “Omission Bias.” Buying feels riskier than not buying because action carries blame if it backfires. Amazon and others flip that bias by showing the cost of doing nothing. When inaction feels like “the bad decision” - whether it's a higher cart price, lost ROI, or missed deal - spending suddenly feels like the safer move.

articles.title: HOW STRUGGLE BECOMES STRATEGY
articles.summary: Pejman Nozad reflects on his 32-year journey from living above a yogurt shop to becoming a top Silicon Valley investor, framing early hardship as the foundation of empathy and insight in venture capital. He highlights lessons on perseverance, resourcefulness, and the power of community in supporting founders through isolation and uncertainty. His message centers on transforming struggle into strategy and using personal adversity as an enduring advantage in investing and leadership.

articles.title: TUSK
articles.summary: AI testing tool for API testing, unit testing, and integration testing

articles.title: CLARIFY
articles.summary: Clarify is an autonomous CRM that automates pipeline building, deal tracking, and sales tasks for founders and early-stage teams.

articles.title: INTEGRAL
articles.summary: Integral is a modern Slack/Discord alternative built for large teams and communities, focused on deeper discussions and fewer interruptions.

articles.title: PRIORITIZATION STARTS WITH STRATEGY
articles.summary: Most teams treat prioritization as a to-do list problem when it's really a strategy problem. Every decision about what's “valuable” reflects a bet on where leverage lives and where decay is setting in. The real work is mapping the forces at play: what increases or protects revenue, what reduces or protects cost, and which advantages will actually last. Once you see those dynamics, prioritization stops being about urgency and starts being about power.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC IS CATCHING UP WITH OPENAI
articles.summary: Anthropic quietly went from $1B to nearly $7B in run-rate revenue this year, a six-fold jump that puts it within striking distance of OpenAI. While OpenAI dominates headlines, Anthropic's API-driven business is growing faster and more efficiently, relying less on free users and more on enterprise integrations. Beneath the public fights about AI ethics and regulation, the real story is simpler: Anthropic might actually win on execution.

articles.title: TWELVE YEARS, 400 STARTUPS, AND A NEW $87,654,321 FUND
articles.summary: Boost VC launches an $87M Fund 4 to invest in 150 Deep Tech startups, focusing on nuclear energy, genetics, and space travel. The fund boosts Boost VC's assets under management to over $300M, with notable investments in companies like Coinbase, Radiant Nuclear, and Colossal Biosciences. Previous funds have shown strong returns, with Fund I achieving a 4.35× DPI and 7.02× TVPI.

articles.title: HIDDEN AI
articles.summary: “Hidden AI” is when the tech stays invisible while transforming cost structures underneath.

articles.title: AI AGENT TOOLING PROVIDER LANGCHAIN RAISES $125M AT $1.25B VALUATION
articles.summary: LangChain raised $125M at a $1.25B valuation in a Series B led by IVP, with investments from CapitalG, ServiceNow Ventures, and others.

articles.title: ANDREESSEN HOROWITZ DPI NUMBERS SHOW PRE-2015 VINTAGES & CRYPTO DRIVING RETURNS
articles.summary: Andreessen Horowitz's pre-2015 and crypto funds drove strong returns, with its first crypto fund achieving a 5x DPI.
